fields.description:
## Why
`_acquire_fcntl` (`src/rebar/_store/lock.py:127-140`) catches EVERY `OSError` from
`fcntl.flock(LOCK_EX|LOCK_NB)` identically: it sleeps and ultimately returns the timeout
sentinel `-1`. So EINTR (an interrupted syscall) and genuine system faults
(ENOLCK/EIO/EBADF) are indistinguishable from real contention (EAGAIN/EACCES) and get masked
as a spurious 30-60s `LockTimeout`. The mkdir leg already branches on `errno`
(`lock.py:222-230`). VALIDATED: no direct test of `_acquire_fcntl`.

## Caller-impact analysis
`_acquire_fcntl` has ONE caller — `acquire` (`lock.py:288`), which maps `fd < 0 → raise
LockTimeout`. The only external `LockTimeout` handler is `inbound_translate.py:258`
(contention on the reconciler write path). After this change: contention (EAGAIN/EACCES)
still returns `-1 → LockTimeout` (behavior UNCHANGED, so that handler is unaffected); EINTR
is retried internally and never surfaces as a failure; only a genuine unexpected fault
(ENOLCK/EIO/EBADF) now propagates as `OSError` (with its errno) instead of a masked
`LockTimeout`. No caller catches `LockTimeout` to specifically handle a system fault, so
surfacing the real error is strictly more informative and does not change contention
handling.

## What
Branch `_acquire_fcntl` on `errno`:
- **EAGAIN / EACCES (contention):** retry until `deadline`, then close the fd and return `-1`
  (unchanged).
- **EINTR (interrupted syscall):** retry the `flock` immediately WITHOUT the 0.05s
  contention sleep (it is not contention, so it must not burn the wait budget).
- **Any other errno (ENOLCK/EIO/EBADF/…):** close the fd and re-raise the `OSError`
  immediately, preserving its errno/identity.
The fd opened at the top is closed on EVERY non-success exit (timeout return and the
re-raise path); on success the caller owns and closes it (unchanged).

Add direct tests of `_acquire_fcntl` that inject specific errnos by monkeypatching the
module's `fcntl.flock`, and spy on `time.sleep` to prove the EINTR path does not sleep.

## Scope
- `src/rebar/_store/lock.py` — errno branching in `_acquire_fcntl`; close fd on all paths.
- `tests/unit/test_lock_stale_reclaim.py` — new direct `_acquire_fcntl` errno tests.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] EAGAIN/EACCES: retries until the deadline, then returns `-1` (contention behavior
      unchanged) — asserted with `flock` always raising EAGAIN and a near-immediate deadline.
- [ ] EINTR: the `flock` is retried and eventually succeeds WITHOUT calling `time.sleep`
      (not treated as contention-budget exhaustion) — asserted with `flock` raising EINTR N
      times then succeeding, and a `time.sleep` spy that records zero calls.
- [ ] A non-contention errno (e.g. ENOLCK) is re-raised immediately as `OSError` with that
      errno (not masked as `-1`/`LockTimeout`) — asserted with `flock` raising ENOLCK.
- [ ] The fd is closed on every failure path (timeout return and re-raise) — asserted by
      spying on the module's `os.close` and confirming the opened fd was closed.
- [ ] `make test` green.
